Buckets.Name: westmark-data-eu-west-1
Buckets._note: Customer data lake. Correctly in eu-west-1 (also a GDPR data-residency requirement). Healthy during the outage.

Buckets.Name: westmark-cd-artifacts
Buckets._note: Build artifacts for CD. Healthy during the outage.

Buckets.Name: westmark-docs-static
Buckets._note: PROBLEM: Origin for the docs CloudFront distribution. Created in 2022 when the company was US-only and never migrated. Was unreachable during the us-east-1 outage; CloudFront cache hits worked for ~75 minutes, then origin fetches returned 502.

Buckets.Name: westmark-cloudtrail-archive
Buckets._note: Long-term CloudTrail log archive. Correctly in eu-west-1.

Buckets.Name: westmark-tf-state
Buckets._note: PROBLEM: Terraform remote state bucket. Created when the platform team set up Terraform in 2023; us-east-1 was the default region in their AWS CLI profile at the time. Could not be accessed during the outage, which would have blocked terraform plan/apply runs even if STS had been working.

_summary_for_player: Two buckets are in us-east-1 (westmark-docs-static and westmark-tf-state). Both were created when defaults pointed at us-east-1 and never migrated. The docs bucket caused customer-visible impact (docs site 502s); the Terraform state bucket would have blocked any infrastructure change during the outage even if the SRE had fixed the STS endpoint issue.
